channel.title: About on Marzena Karpinska
channel.description: Recent content in About on Marzena Karpinska

item.title: CV
item.description: The longer story: long The very short story: very short

item.title: Projects
item.description: Here is a couple of long-term projects I’m working on right now! NoCha Large language models have made huge strides, expanding from handling up to 2k tokens to even 2M tokens. However, evaluating them remains a challenge. It's tough to assess book-length inputs and avoid testing on training data. NoCha addresses this by involving readers who have read recently published novels for entertainment. These readers create true and false claims about the books they read, writing pairs of statements about the same event or character, differing just by the false information included in the false claim.

item.title: Publications
item.description: You can also find my articles on my Google Scholar profile. Total: 20 In English（英語） EMNLP 1. book-open github globe "One Thousand and One Pairs: A "novel" challenge for long-context language models" Marzena Karpinska, Katherine Thai, Kyle Lo, Tanya Goyal, Mohit Iyyer (2024). (to appear at EMNLP 2024) Preprint 2. book-open github globe "CaLMQA: Exploring culturally specific long-form question answering across 23 languages" Shane Arora*, Marzena Karpinska*, Hung-Ting Chen, Ipsita Bhattacharjee, Mohit Iyyer, Eunsol Choi (2024).
